title: Treasury Securities - Federal Reserve Bank of New York

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This is an informational institutional page describing the New York Fed's role in Treasury securities operations for the System Open Market Account (SOMA). The page explains that the Open Market Trading Desk purchases and sells Treasury securities as directed by the FOMC, historically to match currency circulation and more recently to implement monetary policy. The Desk executes transactions with primary dealers and also provides limited transaction services to approximately 200 central banks and official institutions.
key_points: The New York Fed's Open Market Trading Desk conducts Treasury outright purchases and sales, and rollovers, as directed by the FOMC
Transactions are executed with primary dealers in the secondary market
Since end of 2008, the FOMC has directed Treasury portfolio expansion on several occasions to implement monetary policy
The Desk also provides transaction services to approximately 200 central banks, governments, and official institutions
Recent statements referenced include FedTrade operations updates from Mar 12, 2026 and Feb 23, 2026
why_it_matters: This page provides institutional context on how the Federal Reserve conducts Treasury market operations through the SOMA portfolio. While not a policy decision or data release, it describes the operational framework that affects Treasury market liquidity and the Fed's balance sheet implementation. Relevant for understanding Fed market structure and plumbing.
market_structure_context: Describes the operational framework for Fed Treasury operations: (1) secondary market purchases/sales with primary dealers, (2) SOMA portfolio rollovers at auction, (3) transaction services for official sector account holders (~200 central banks/governments). The page references recent statements on FedTrade operations (Mar 12, 2026) and Treasury small value exercises (Feb 23, 2026).
important_numbers: 200 central banks, governments, and official institutions served
